volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Philoctetes (Section 1407a - Section 1470)

sections.label: Section 1407a
sections.translation: — I will keep them away .

sections.label: Section 1407b
sections.translation: Then say your farewell to this island , and leave with me .

sections.label: Section 1408
sections.original: μήπω γε , πρὶν ἂν τῶν ἡμετέρων
sections.translation: Not yet , not until you have heard my commands ,

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.translation: son of Poeas . Know that your ears perceive the voice of Heracles , and that you look upon his face . For your sake I have left my divine seat and come

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: τὰ Διός τε φράσων βουλεύματά σοι
sections.translation: to reveal to you the purposes of Zeus , and to halt the journey on which you are departing . Hearken to my words . First I would tell you of my own fortunes — how , by toiling through and enduring so many toils to the end , I have won the glory of deathlessness , as you witness .

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: ἀθάνατον ἀρετὴν ἔσχον , ὡς πάρεσθʼ ὁρᾶν .
sections.translation:
And for you , be sure , this fate is ordained , that through these toils of yours you will make your life far-famed .
You shall go with this man to the Trojan city , where , first , you shall be healed of your cruel sickness ,

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.translation: and then , chosen out as foremost among the warriors in prowess , with my bow you shall sever Paris , the author of these evils , from life . You shall sack Troy and shall receive from the army the spoils of supreme valor to carry home

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: Ποίαντι πατρὶ πρὸς πάτρας Οἴτης πλάκα .
sections.translation: to the heights of your native Oeta for the delight of your father Poeas . And whatever spoils you receive from that army , from them carry to my pyre a thank-offering for my bow . And these counsels hold for you also , son of Achilles ,

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: ἑλεῖν τὸ Τροίας πεδίον οὔθʼ οὗτος σέθεν .
sections.translation: for you have not the might to subdue the Trojan realm without him , nor he without you . Rather , like twin lions with the same quarry , each of you must guard the other ’ s life . For the healing of your sickness , I will send Asclepius to Troy , since it is doomed to fall a second time

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: τόξοις ἁλῶναι . τοῦτο δʼ ἐννοεῖθʼ , ὅταν
sections.translation: before my arrows . But of this be mindful , when you plunder the land — that you show reverence towards the gods . Do this because Father Zeus regards all else as of less account , and because Piety does not die along with mortals . Whether they are alive or dead , their piety does not perish .

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.translation: Ah , friend whose voice I have longed to hear , whose shape I see at long last , I will not disobey your commands !

sections.label: Section 1448
sections.translation: I , too , consent .

sections.label: Section 1449
sections.translation: Then do not long delay ,

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.translation: for the occasion and the fair wind there at your stern urge you forward .

sections.label: Section 1452
sections.translation: Come , then , let me hail this land as I depart . Farewell , chamber that shared my watches . Farewell ,

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.translation: nymphs of stream and meadow , and you , strong pounding of the sea-lashed cape , where often in the cavern ’ s inmost recess my head was wetted by the south wind ’ s blasts , and where many times the Hermaean mount sent an echo

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.translation: to my sad groans in the gale of my sorrow ! But now , clear springs and Lycian fount , I am leaving you , leaving you at last , though such a hope had never buoyed me ! Farewell , sea-wrapped Lemnos ,

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.translation: and send me off with sailing fair to my heart ’ s content , send me to the destination appointed me by mighty Fate and the will of my friends , and by the all-taming god who has brought these things to pass .

sections.label: Section 1469
sections.translation: Now let us all leave together ,

sections.label: Section 1470
sections.translation: once we have prayed to the nymphs of the sea to come be the guides of our safe return .
